claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about your child's age:**

How old is the child you're claiming Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know which category applies to your child. If you have multiple children, we can go through each one - just start with the first child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who's 7 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information! Alex, at 7 years old, falls into the "under 16" category, which is great news for eligibility purposes. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Since Alex is only 7 years old, this question about working hours is straightforward - children this young wouldn't be in paid employment of 24+ hours per week.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:** Is Alex receiving any benefits in their own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?

This would be quite unusual for a 7-year-old, but I need to confirm. Is Alex **not** receiving any such benefits in their own name?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name. They're only 7, so nothing like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That makes perfect sense - at 7 years old, that's completely expected. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Since Alex is only 7 years old, they wouldn't be in any kind of apprenticeship - those are for older teenagers and young adults. I'll move forward accordingly.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now I need to check your residency status.**

Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements? You only need to meet **one** of these:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You are a resident living in the UK
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. **Pre-settled status and seeking work** - You have pre-settled status and are actively looking for work
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status and can support yourself financially (without claiming certain benefits)
6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status and are a student with sufficient resources
7. **Pre-settled status as family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You're living abroad as a Crown servant

Which of these applies to you? For most people, it's simply that they **normally live in the UK**.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I don't live in the UK at the moment. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, I need to ask a follow-up question:

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant?**

Crown servants include people who work for the UK government overseas, such as:
- Members of the Armed Forces stationed abroad
- Diplomatic staff at embassies or consulates
- Civil servants posted overseas

If you're not a Crown servant, or don't fall into one of the other categories I mentioned (such as having pre-settled status with various qualifying conditions), you may not meet the residency requirements for Child Benefit.

Could you tell me more about your situation? Are you a Crown servant, or do you have any connection to the EU Settlement Scheme (settled or pre-settled status)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I'm just living abroad at the moment - nothing to do with government work or the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, based on what you've told me, it appears you don't meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, I need to report the outcome of this assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment Result

### ❌ Not Eligible

**Child:** Alex (age 7)

**Outcome:** Unfortunately, you are **not currently eligible** for Child Benefit.

---

### Why?

While Alex meets all the requirements for a qualifying child (under 16, not working full-time, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship), **you do not meet the UK residency requirements**.

To claim Child Benefit, you must:
- Normally live in the UK, **or**
- Have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying conditions), **or**
- Be a Crown servant living abroad

Since you're living abroad without any of these qualifying circumstances, you cannot claim Child Benefit at this time.

---

### What You Can Do

1. **If your circumstances change** - If you return to live in the UK, you may become eligible and can apply at that point.

2. **Check for other support** - If you're living in another country, you may want to check whether that country offers family benefits you could claim instead.

3. **Get further advice** - If you think your situation might qualify under one of the special categories, you can contact HMRC directly for guidance: [www.gov.uk/child-benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit)

I'm sorry I couldn't deliver better news. Is there anything else you'd like me to clarify about Child Benefit eligibility?

reasonings: Alex is 7 years old, which would normally qualify as a child under 16. Alex is not working 24+ hours per week, does not receive benefits in their own right, and is not in any apprenticeship. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is currently living abroad and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and does not meet any of the other qualifying residency paths. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 7), who would otherwise be a qualifying child for Child Benefit purposes. However, the claimant does not meet the residency requirements as they are living abroad without any qualifying circumstances (not a Crown servant, no EU Settlement Scheme status). UK residency is a fundamental requirement for claiming Child Benefit.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

The claimant is currently living abroad and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and does not meet any of the other qualifying residency paths. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

However, the claimant does not meet the residency requirements as they are living abroad without any qualifying circumstances (not a Crown servant, no EU Settlement Scheme status).
